abortRestart: You can restart the wizard at any time.
abortRestored: The strip has been restored to its previous state.
aboutInfo: This adapter is free and open source. If you find it useful, consider supporting the development.
canYouSeeStrip: Can you see the light on the strip?
cloudInfo: Enter your Govee API key to enable device names, scenes and segment control. Get your key in the Govee Home app under Account > Settings > About Us > Apply for API Key.
cloudSnapshotDesc: Snapshots you saved in the Govee Home app. Selecting one replays that state on the device.
deleteLocalSnapshotDesc: Write a local snapshot name to delete it. Does not affect Govee Home app snapshots.
deviceTierSeed: Seed — beta, needs experimental toggle in adapter settings
deviceTierUnknown: Unknown SKU — please run diag.export and post in a GitHub issue
errAlreadyActive: Wizard already active for {name}. Please abort first.
errAnswerFirst: Please answer at least once first (Yes visible or No dark).
errNoSegments: {name} has no segments — wizard not applicable.
errNoWizard: No wizard active. Please click 'Start' first.
experimentalQuirksInfo: Govee keeps releasing new models. Some are already prepared in the adapter code but not confirmed by any tester yet. This switch enables them. Which devices these are and whether yours is among them is documented in the Wiki.
experimentalQuirksTooltip: Activates devices with status ⚪ Experimental from devices.json. They may work, return wrong values, or do nothing. The Wiki page Devices lists every entry and its status.
idle: No wizard active. Pick an LED strip above and click ▶ Start.
lanInfo: Devices with LAN mode enabled in the Govee Home app are discovered automatically. LAN is the fastest control channel.
localSnapshotDesc: Snapshots saved by this adapter on the ioBroker server. Independent of the Govee Home app.
logIdleTimeout: Segment wizard for {name}: idle timeout (5 min), aborted
manualSegmentsDesc: Enable manual segment list (e.g. for cut LED strips with fewer physical segments than reported)
mqttAuthAccountLocked: Govee account temporarily locked — open the Govee Home app and check the status.
mqttAuthCodeInvalid: Two-factor code invalid or expired — please request a new code.
mqttAuthCodeSent: Code sent to your Govee email address (check the spam folder).
mqttAuthEmailNotRegistered: This email is not registered with Govee.
mqttAuthLoginNoMqtt: Login accepted, but the MQTT connection is not up yet — restart the adapter.
mqttAuthLoginOk: Login successful — real-time status updates active.
mqttAuthNeedCredentials: Email + password required in the adapter settings.
mqttAuthRateLimited: Govee reports a rate limit — please try again later.
mqttAuthThrottled: Please wait %ss — a code was just requested.
mqttAuthVerifyRequired: Govee requires two-factor confirmation. Click 'Request verification code', enter the code from the email and save.
mqttInfo: Enter your Govee account credentials to enable real-time status updates via MQTT. Without this, status is only updated on request.
networkInterfaceTooltip: Select the network interface for LAN device discovery. Leave empty to use all interfaces (recommended for most setups).
refreshCloudDesc: Write true to re-fetch scenes, snapshots and capabilities for this device from the Govee Cloud. Use after creating a new snapshot in the Govee Home app.
saveLocalSnapshotDesc: Write a name to save the current device state (power, brightness, colour, per-segment colours) as a new local snapshot.
twoFaCodeTooltip: One-time code from the Govee email. Cleared automatically after a successful login.
twoFaHeader: 2-Factor verification (only if Govee asks for it)
twoFaInfo: If the adapter log shows 'Verification required', click 'Request code', enter the code from the Govee email below, and save.
validatorEmailError: Please enter a valid email address
wizardHelp: How it works: the current segment flashes WHITE, all others stay dark. Click 'Yes, visible' or 'No, dark'. When you've reached the physical end of the strip, click 'Done – end of strip'. The wizard determines the real segment count and, if gaps were found, saves a manual_list automatically. Session auto-aborts after 5 minutes of inactivity.
wizardInfo: Measures the real length of your LED strip and detects gaps (on cut strips). Pick the strip, click Start, and for each flashing segment answer: visible, dark, or 'Done – end of strip'.
yesNoDoneLine: → Yes, visible   or   → No, dark   or   → Done – end of strip
